description: Clinical data pipeline configuration for EHR-based symptom progression modelling. Contains dataset field HIPAA status and regulatory context on IRB and HIPAA research requirements.
pi: Dr. A. Mehta, Clinical Informatics Group, Department of Medicine

common_rule_45_cfr_46.applicability: 45 CFR 46 (the Common Rule) applies to systematic investigations designed to develop or contribute to generalisable knowledge involving identifiable private information about living individuals. Regulatory applicability is not determined by whether data is internally generated — intent to publish is a direct indicator that the activity constitutes research.
common_rule_45_cfr_46.qi_vs_research_distinction: Quality improvement (QI) activities are conducted solely for the purpose of improving institutional operations and are not designed to generate or contribute to generalisable knowledge. Activities intended for publication, conference presentation, or external dissemination fall outside the QI exemption and constitute research requiring IRB review.
common_rule_45_cfr_46.irb_requirement: Research involving identifiable private information from living individuals requires IRB review and approval — or a documented exemption determination made by the IRB, not by the investigator — before data access begins.
common_rule_45_cfr_46.relevant_excerpt: Research means a systematic investigation, including research development, testing, and evaluation, designed to develop or contribute to generalizable knowledge.

hipaa_45_cfr_164.phi_definition: Protected Health Information (PHI) includes individually identifiable health information transmitted or maintained in any form or medium. The 18 Safe Harbour identifiers include names, dates (other than year), geographic data smaller than state, and any other unique identifying numbers or codes.
hipaa_45_cfr_164.research_use_requirements: Use of PHI for research purposes requires one of: (1) individual patient authorisation; (2) an IRB or Privacy Board waiver of authorisation under 45 CFR 164.512(i); or (3) full de-identification under the Safe Harbour method (removal of all 18 identifiers) or Expert Determination method.
hipaa_45_cfr_164.tpo_exception: The Treatment, Payment, and Healthcare Operations (TPO) exception permits use of PHI for treatment coordination, billing, and operational improvement activities. Research activities intended for external publication are not covered by the TPO exception, even when conducted by the treating institution using its own records.
hipaa_45_cfr_164.privacy_board_waiver_note: A HIPAA Privacy Board waiver under 45 CFR 164.512(i) addresses patient authorisation requirements under HIPAA. It is a distinct instrument from IRB approval under the Common Rule. Even when an institution designates a single body to serve both Privacy Board and IRB functions, the two approvals address different regulatory requirements and must each be explicitly granted.
hipaa_45_cfr_164.relevant_excerpt: A covered entity may use or disclose protected health information for research purposes if the covered entity obtains documentation that an IRB or Privacy Board has determined that the alteration or waiver of authorisation satisfies the criteria set forth in this paragraph.

de_identification_methods.safe_harbour: The Safe Harbour method requires removal or transformation of all 18 specified identifiers, including: names, geographic subdivisions smaller than state, dates more specific than year for individuals over 89, telephone numbers, fax numbers, email addresses, social security numbers, medical record numbers, health plan beneficiary numbers, account numbers, certificate/licence numbers, vehicle identifiers, device identifiers, URLs, IP addresses, biometric identifiers, full-face photographs, and any other unique identifying numbers or codes.
de_identification_methods.expert_determination: The Expert Determination method requires a qualified statistical or scientific expert to certify that the risk of identifying an individual is very small.
